mnemonics: API-CAL: APIs Consume Available Limits. Think of APIs as guests consuming resources at a party, and capacity as the available food and space.
keyPoints: Every API call consumes server resources (CPU, memory, network I/O).
Unexpected spikes in API traffic can lead to performance bottlenecks and service degradation.
Capacity planning must include monitoring and forecasting API usage trends to scale resources appropriately.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on user traffic while ignoring the load generated by backend and third-party API integrations.
Failing to implement rate limiting and throttling, leading to system overload from a few bad actors or misconfigured clients.
Not planning for the 'waterbed effect,' where optimizing one API's performance simply shifts the capacity bottleneck to another part of the system.
